name: Satyanarayan Puja
benefit: A devotional ritual to Lord Vishnu invoking truth, prosperity, and well-being. Most performed for new ventures and housewarmings.
what: A thanksgiving and wish-fulfillment ritual to Lord Vishnu, centered on the 5-chapter Satyanarayan Katha (sacred story). Performed for new ventures, housewarmings, and milestones.
when: Purnima or Ekadashi preferred · Thursdays auspicious · 1.5–2 hrs

steps.en: Declaration of intent — your wish, spoken aloud
steps.detail: Holding water, rice and a flower in your right palm, you state your name, family, place and the wish itself. The pandit guides the words — you repeat line by line.
Every puja opens with Ganesha. The pandit invokes him into a betel nut or small idol so no obstacles interrupt the main ritual.
A copper pot of water, topped with five mango leaves and a coconut, is set on a bed of rice — the deity's seat for the ceremony.
The deity image is bathed with panchamrit — milk, curd, ghee, honey, sugar — then rinsed with clean water and dressed.

steps.en: Listening to all 5 chapters of the sacred story
steps.detail: The heart of the puja: the family sits and listens to all five chapters of the Satyanarayan story (about 30–40 minutes). Listening itself is the vow.
Lamps are waved in slow circles before the deity while everyone stands for the aarti song — bells and claps are welcome.
The sheera (semolina halwa) offered during the katha is shared with everyone present — accepting it completes the vrat.

essential: Panchamrit — milk, curd, ghee, honey, sugar
optional: New yellow or red cloth · वस्त्र

samagriNote: haldi-kumkum & akshat usually at home already

art.alt: Lord Vishnu seated on Shesha with Lakshmi and Saraswati — vintage oleograph
art.caption: Vishnu on Shesha, with Lakshmi & Saraswati
art.credit: Raja Ravi Varma oleograph, c. 1900 · Wellcome Collection · public domain

name: Griha Pravesh & Vastu Shanti
benefit: A housewarming ceremony performed before the family sleeps in the new home, appeasing Vastu Purusha and inviting lasting peace into the space.
what: The housewarming ceremony performed before first sleeping in a new home; Vastu Shanti appeases the deity of the site and directions.
when: Uttarayan period favored · avoid Adhik Maas & Shraadh · Abhijit Muhurat common · 2–4 hrs

steps.detail: Before anyone enters, the main door is decorated with a toran and honored with kumkum, rice and a flower — the threshold is worshipped first.
Ganesha is invoked first so the move into the new home begins with obstacles cleared.
The couple carries the kalash into the house — right foot across the threshold first — and sets it at the puja spot.

steps.en: Offerings to the spirit of the site
steps.detail: Offerings to Vastu Purusha, the spirit of the site, ask acceptance for the new household and lasting harmony in the space.
The nine planetary deities are honored with nine grains (navadhanya) so no graha casts a shadow over the household.

steps.en: Milk boiled till it overflows — abundance
steps.detail: Milk is boiled in a new vessel until it spills over — on purpose. An overflowing home is the whole point.
Ghee, sesame and samidha wood are offered into the sacred fire, closing with the purnahuti — the final, complete offering.

steps.en: Closing lamp ritual; feeding the pandit
steps.detail: A closing aarti, then the pandit and guests eat first — the family eats only after they are fed.

mantra.note: The completion mantra, at havan close

essential: Copper or brass kalash — never steel
Coconut wrapped in red cloth + mouli thread
Havan kit — samidha wood, ghee, sesame, bel

samagriNote: the havan kit is the one item most shops pre-bundle

art.caption: Lakshmi-Narayan on Garuda
art.credit: Ravi Varma Press chromolithograph · public domain

name: Ganesh Puja
benefit: Worship of Lord Ganesha, remover of obstacles and lord of beginnings — performed before any new venture.
what: Worship of Lord Ganesha, remover of obstacles and lord of beginnings — before any new venture, exam, or journey.
when: Wednesdays favored · Ganesh Chaturthi (Sep 14, 2026) festival peak · 45 min–1.5 hrs

steps.detail: The pandit chants the invocation asking Ganesha to be present in the clay idol; you welcome him with a flower at the feet.

steps.en: Symbolic bath with water, milk, chandan
steps.detail: The idol is gently bathed — water, then milk, then chandan water — and wiped dry with a fresh cloth.
Sandalwood paste goes on the forehead, unbroken rice grains at the feet — the honored-guest treatment.
Handfuls of red hibiscus and 21 blades of durva grass, Ganesha's signature offerings, are placed at the feet.
Twenty-one modaks are offered; one is touched to the idol's mouth, the rest come back to you as prasad.
You circle the deity clockwise — 1, 3 or 21 times — and close with the aarti, lamps circling as everyone sings.

samagriNote: durva grass matters — Ganesha's signature offering

art.alt: Lord Ganesha enthroned with Riddhi and Siddhi — vintage chromolithograph
art.caption: Ganesha with Riddhi & Siddhi
art.credit: Ravi Varma Press chromolithograph · public domain

name: Lakshmi-Ganesh Puja
benefit: Joint worship of Goddess Lakshmi (wealth) and Lord Ganesha (obstacle removal) — performed by business owners for cash-flow revival, every Friday or at Diwali.
what: Joint worship of Lakshmi (wealth) and Ganesha (obstacles) — Ganesha first, so nothing blocks Lakshmi's blessings. The business owner's ritual. Placement rule: viewer's left = Ganesha, right = Lakshmi.
when: Diwali night is the peak (Nov 8, 2026) · any Friday for ongoing prosperity · 1–1.5 hrs

steps.en: Declaration of intent — your wish, spoken aloud
steps.detail: With water and rice in your palm, you state the family's wish for the business in plain words; the pandit shapes it into the formal vow.
Ganesha first, always — placed to the viewer's left — so the path is clear before Lakshmi is welcomed.

steps.en: Pot on red cloth with rice
steps.detail: The kalash sits on red cloth over rice, and a coin is dropped into the water — wealth given a seat in the home.

steps.en: Invoking the goddess into idol or coin
steps.detail: Lakshmi is invoked into the idol (or a silver coin) on the right; lotus or marigold flowers are offered as she arrives.
Both deities are bathed in panchamrit, rinsed, dried and dressed in fresh cloth.
Sweets, fruits and puffed rice (lava) are offered — business families also place the account books or shop keys before her.

steps.en: Rows of lamps — Lakshmi never enters a dark house
steps.detail: Rows of diyas are lit before the final aarti — light in every corner, since Lakshmi never enters a dark house.

samagriNote: many diyas — rows of lamps are the point

art.alt: Goddess Lakshmi standing on a lotus — Raja Ravi Varma, 1896
art.caption: Goddess Lakshmi

name: Maha Mrityunjaya Puja
benefit: A healing ritual to Lord Shiva, 'conqueror of death,' chanted for recovery, protection, and long life.
what: A healing and protection ritual to Lord Shiva as 'conqueror of death' — repeated chanting (jaap) for recovery from illness, before surgery, or for longevity.
when: Mondays · Pradosh · Shravan month · early morning (Brahma Muhurta) ideal · 1–2 hrs for 108 chants

steps.detail: The family states the purpose — recovery, protection, longevity — and for whom. The person prayed for does not need to be present.

steps.en: Fasting and bathing before the ritual
steps.detail: The performers bathe and keep a light fast; the space is cleaned and sprinkled with gangajal before the ritual begins.
The linga is bathed in a continuous stream — water, milk, panchamrit — while bel leaves are laid on top, Shiva's essential offering.

steps.en: 108 chants counted on a rudraksha mala
steps.detail: The core of the ritual: 108 repetitions of the Mahamrityunjaya mantra counted on a rudraksha mala. Family may chant along or sit in silence.
A lamp offering to Shiva with the aarti song closes the chanting.
The abhishek water and blessed food are shared — a portion is set aside for the person the puja was performed for.

mantra.dev: ॐ त्र्यम्बकं यजामहे सुगन्धिं पुष्टिवर्धनम् । उर्वारुकमिव बन्धनान्मृत्योर्मुक्षीय मामृतात् ॥
mantra.iast: Om Tryambakaṁ Yajāmahe Sugandhiṁ Puṣṭivardhanam, Urvārukamiva Bandhanān Mṛtyor Mukṣīya Māmṛtāt
mantra.note: Rigveda · protection from illness and untimely death

essential: Panchamrit set — milk, curd, ghee, honey, sugar

samagriNote: bel leaves are essential for Shiva worship

art.alt: Shankar — Shiva with Parvati, Ganesha and Nandi — vintage chromolithograph
art.caption: Shankar — Shiva with Parvati & Ganesha
art.credit: Ravi Varma Press, Malavli, c. 1910 · public domain
